prompt: Extreme close-up macro of a tiny translucent cephalopod hatchling, 15mm long, suspended in dark water. Its body is almost completely clear like glass, but the skin SHIMMERS with iridescent structural color — blues, greens, cool silver-violets like an opal or oil-on-water — NO amber pigment yet, only iridescence. TWO TALL THIN EYE STALKS like garden snail eyes rise from its head, disproportionately long compared to the tiny body, each tipped with a round dark glowing eye. Eight fine tentacles trail below. A jewel, not a lantern. Deep black background. Macro detail. NOT a snail.
